author: Dr. Allyson Pishko (Hematology)
content:
HEMATOLOGY CONSULT — VTE MANAGEMENT

Reason for consult: Management of acute unprovoked PE + extensive DVT in a previously healthy 43-year-old, complicated by a concurrent liver mass concerning for malignancy.

DATA REVIEWED (this is a framing/data-gathering note; specific timing decisions to be made with the primary team and proceduralists):
- VTE: acute segmental PE (low-risk — troponin negative, no RV strain) with extensive right-leg DVT (proximal femoral, posterior tibial, GSV). Essentially UNPROVOKED.
- Currently anticoagulated with a heparin infusion (short-acting agent chosen given the anticipated need to characterize/biopsy the liver lesion).
- Liver lesion: heterogeneous right-lobe enhancement on CTPA; dedicated MRI pending/just resulted (large right-lobe lesion + left-lobe foci + apparent right portal-vein thrombus; radiology with high suspicion for malignancy and a recommendation to consider needle biopsy). LFTs near-normal with preserved synthetic function; tumor markers nonspecific.
- Renal function normal; weight >60 kg (relevant to DOAC dosing).

FRAMING (A&P intentionally brief):
#Acute VTE requiring uninterrupted therapeutic anticoagulation. The competing consideration is the recommended liver biopsy, which would require interrupting anticoagulation.
#The central management tension — how to sequence robust anticoagulation against the timing of a diagnostic biopsy — will be worked out with the primary team, interventional radiology, and hepatology.
#Plan to transition to an oral agent (apixaban) for outpatient management; biopsy to be arranged on an outpatient basis. Specific timing/interruption details to be determined.
#Occult-malignancy framing acknowledged given the unprovoked VTE + liver mass; this informs anticoagulation duration discussions down the line.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Allyson Pishko, MD (Hematology). 7/7/2027.
